channel.title: Nature Communications
channel.description:
Nature Communications is an open access, multidisciplinary journal dedicated to publishing high-quality research in all areas of the biological, health, physical, chemical and Earth sciences. Papers published by the journal aim to represent important advances of significance to specialists within each field.

We are committed to providing an efficient service for both authors and readers. Our team of independent editors make rapid and fair publication decisions. Prompt dissemination of accepted papers to a wide readership and beyond is achieved through a programme of continuous online publication.
channel.dc:rights: © 2026 Macmillan Publishers Limited, part of Springer Nature. All rights reserved.
channel.prism:copyright: © 2026 Macmillan Publishers Limited, part of Springer Nature. All rights reserved.

item.title: Layer-selective hydrogenation and proton transport in twisted bilayer graphene
item.content:encoded:
Nature Communications, Published online: 04 April 2026; doi:10.1038/s41467-026-71147-y

Authors demonstrate layer-selective conductor–insulator transitions in twisted bilayer graphene driven by hydrogenation, realising an electrochemical interface with two decoupled 2D electron gases that enables configurable logic gates.
item.dc:title: Layer-selective hydrogenation and proton transport in twisted bilayer graphene
item.dc:creator: J. Tong
G. Chen
H. Li
E. Hoenig
M. Alhashmi
X. Zhang
D. Bahamon
G. R. Tainton
S. Sullivan-Allsop
Y. Mayamei
D. R. da Costa
L. F. Vega
S. J. Haigh
D. Domaretskiy
F. M. Peeters
M. Lozada-Hidalgo

item.title: Realizing Landau levels with open equifrequency contours around band singularities
item.content:encoded:
Nature Communications, Published online: 04 April 2026; doi:10.1038/s41467-026-70879-1

Landau levels have been observed in systems only with closed equifrequency contours (EFCs) around band singularities, i.e. type-I Dirac or Weyl systems. Here, we demonstrate the existence of Landau levels with open EFCs around band singularities through combined action of artificial electric and magnetic fields, whereas they cannot be generated by B only.
item.dc:title: Realizing Landau levels with open equifrequency contours around band singularities
item.dc:creator: Cuicui Lu
Oubo You
Yanji Zheng
Shaojie Ma
Qingdong Yang
Hsun-Chi Chan
Zhihao Wang
Wenshuo Ma
Yong-Chun Liu
Shuang Zhang

item.title: Non-toxic silver telluride colloidal quantum dot mid-infrared photodetector
item.content:encoded:
Nature Communications, Published online: 04 April 2026; doi:10.1038/s41467-026-71374-3

Eom et al. report the growth of Ag2Te colloidal quantum dots for mid-infrared photodetectors covering the 3–5 μm infrared range. The detector shows a rise/fall time of 211/523 ns and a noise-equivalent temperature difference of 0.3 K, which has potential for reliable diagnosis of fever-level body temperatures.
item.dc:title: Non-toxic silver telluride colloidal quantum dot mid-infrared photodetector
item.dc:creator: So Young Eom
Jin Hyeok Lee
Haemin Song
Suheon Son
Kwang Seob Jeong
